ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers green tea as a category. No specific code for the Ayataka commercial bottled variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers green tea as a substance category; no specific code for the Ayataka commercial bottled product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of Ayataka Japanese Green Tea, as typically sold in Singapore convenience stores and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka is a commercially bottled unsweetened Japanese green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan. No branded USDA entry exists for Ayataka specifically. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), which represents unsweetened brewed green tea and is the closest approved-source match. The product is unsweetened; sugar and carbohydrate values are effectively zero or trace. Sodium is minimal. Values are consistent with the Ayataka product label profile (0 kcal, 0 g sugar, 0 g fat, 0 g protein per 100 ml as reported on the Coca-Cola Japan product page, corroborated by USDA brewed green tea data).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ayataka bottled green tea. Plain unsweetened brewed green tea contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates (approximately 0.2 g per 100 ml). Per University of Sydney GI Database convention and peer-reviewed literature, plain unsweetened tea has an effectively zero glycemic response. glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) as this beverage contains no meaningful glycemic carbohydrates. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 given the trace carbohydrate content per 500 ml serving (approximately 1.0 g total carbohydrate).

hypertension.guidance: Ayataka Japanese Green Tea contains approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml and is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension; regular green tea consumption has been associated with modest blood pressure reduction in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar and negligible glycemic carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka Japanese Green Tea is unsweetened with effectively zero sugar and a glycemic index of 1, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; catechins in green tea may also support insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant catechin content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka Japanese Green Tea contains no fat or cholesterol and provides catechins (EGCG) that have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and cardiovascular risk reduction in prospective cohort studies.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka Japanese Green Tea is a commercially bottled, ready-to-drink unsweetened green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan under the Ayataka brand, which takes its name from a traditional Japanese teahouse aesthetic. The tea is brewed from Camellia sinensis leaves using a method intended to replicate the taste of tea prepared by a skilled tea master, resulting in a mild, slightly astringent, and umami-forward flavour profile characteristic of high-quality Japanese ryokucha (green tea). The beverage is sold in 500 ml and 600 ml PET bottles and is widely available across Japan, Singapore, and other Asian markets. It contains no added sugar, no artificial sweeteners, no fat, and no protein, making it essentially a near-zero-calorie beverage. The primary bioactive compounds are green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which have been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic health benefits. From a clinical nutrition perspective, Ayataka is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible glycemic load, minimal sodium, and absence of saturated fat. It is suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free dietary patterns. In Singapore, it is commonly consumed as a standalone beverage with meals or as a refreshing drink in warm weather, and is available through convenience stores, supermarkets, and food delivery platforms such as field survey.
common_user_queries: Is Ayataka green tea sugar free?
How many calories in Ayataka green tea?

confidence_reason: Ayataka is a well-known commercial product with a publicly documented nutritional profile (0 kcal, 0 g sugar, 0 g fat, 0 g protein per 100 ml per Coca-Cola Japan product labelling). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea data (FDC ID 171917), which closely matches the product profile. Confidence is medium rather than high because no direct USDA Branded Food or HPB Singapore entry exists for this specific commercial product, and the adaptation relies on the close compositional equivalence of unsweetened brewed green tea.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g/ml, reflecting the trace carbohydrate content of brewed green tea; this is effectively negligible and consistent with the product label which rounds to 0 kcal. 2. protein_g is naturally zero as unsweetened brewed green tea contains no protein by nature. 3. fat_g is naturally zero as unsweetened brewed green tea contains no fat by nature. 4. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central brewed green tea data and consistent with the Ayataka product label. 5. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as brewed tea liquid contains no dietary fibre. 6. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 given trace carbohydrate content. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 8. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 9. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 10. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 11. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 13. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 14. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 15. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 16. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy brewed green tea entry. 17. The input cuisine tag was listed as Thai, which appears to be a field survey platform categorisation artefact based on the restaurant (Shrimp Prawn Seafood - Boat Quay). Ayataka is a Japanese commercial product; cuisine has been corrected to Japanese. 18. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no specific commercial product codes exist in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT for Ayataka.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_name from promotional field survey title to canonical English name 'Ayataka Japanese Green Tea'.
Corrected cuisine from 'Thai' (field survey platform artefact) to 'Japanese' (correct product origin).
Added Japanese local name '綾鷹 日本緑茶' to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Japanese cuisine.
Set glycemic_index to 1 per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; documented in notes and gi_source.
Documented all zero macro values with 'naturally zero' and 'confirmed zero' justification phrases in notes.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit source-not-reported justifications in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency; all aliases_by_language strings present verbatim in flat aliases.
Used USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as approved secondary source; adaptation documented in nutrient_source.
